nodes.context: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1979 in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada.
Canadian outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Loners.
Alberta-based outlaw motorcycle club; a Loners chapter patched over to them.
Outlaw motorcycle club, one of the largest in Ontario in the late 1980s.
International outlaw motorcycle club; had a working relationship with the Loners in the 1990s.
Outlaw motorcycle club founded by Frank Lenti after his expulsion from the Loners in 1994.
Ontario outlaw motorcycle club; some Loners joined them after the 1997 split.
Puppet gang of the Hells Angels; members moved to Ontario in 1993.
Puppet gang set up by Hells Angels in Ontario in 1993; quickly collapsed.
Ally of the Loners according to infobox.

Rival of the Loners according to infobox (2012–2015).
International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada in 1979.
Motorcycle club led by Wayne Kellestine and Giovanni Muscedere, absorbed by the Loners in 1999.
Major outlaw motorcycle club, enemy of the Loners during the Ontario Biker War.
Outlaw motorcycle club that fought alongside the Loners against the Hells Angels.
Group of biker clubs in Ireland that the Loners tried to recruit.
Ontario motorcycle club affiliated with the Hells Angels.
Canadian motorcycle club that allied with the Loners and later became Bandidos.
International outlaw motorcycle club that patched over the Rock Machine and had relationships with the Loners.
California-based motorcycle club that some Loners joined in 2007.
American motorcycle club that attempted to expand into Canada, causing conflict with the Loners.
Ontario motorcycle club, neutral during the Ontario Biker War.
Ontario motorcycle club, neutral during the Ontario Biker War.
International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Canada in 1979, with chapters worldwide.
Outlaw motorcycle club, rival of the Loners in Peterborough.
Support club for the Loners, absorbed by them in 2012.
Outlaw motorcycle club that absorbed the Vagos Peterborough chapter in 2015, historically had good relations with the Loners.
Organized crime group mentioned as working in conjunction with the Loners.
International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada in 1979. Has chapters in Canada, Italy, US, and other countries.
Support club for the Loners Motorcycle Club in Canada.
Outlaw motorcycle club, historically allied with Loners but later in conflict.
Outlaw motorcycle club, opposed by Loners and Outlaws during the Ontario Biker War.
Motorcycle club that had conflict with Loners Peterborough chapter in 2012-2014, later absorbed by Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: These patch-overs caused tensions to run high between the two clubs, which would reach a fever pitch with the killing of Brian Beaucage.
edges.context: Tensions and conflict between the Loners and Satan's Choice escalated into open war in 1995.

edges.evidence_quote: However, it would 'Patch-Over' to the Alberta-based Rebels MC.
edges.context: A Loners chapter in Saskatoon patched over to the Rebels MC.

edges.evidence_quote: However, a working relationship was established between the two groups for a time with the Loners agreeing to buy their narcotics from the Angels.
edges.context: The Loners had a cooperative drug supply relationship with the Hells Angels in the 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Loners came into conflict with the Diablos in the summer of 1995, who called upon Satan's Choice for aid.
edges.context: The Loners fought against the Diablos in 1995.

edges.evidence_quote: The Satan's Choice made a firm alliance with the Diablos, led by Loners exile, Frank Lenti.
edges.context: Satan's Choice allied with the Diablos against the Loners.

edges.evidence_quote: The pro-Hells Angel faction led by Grano joined the Para-Dice Riders while a rump remained under Raso.
edges.context: After a split in 1997, a faction of the Loners joined the Para-Dice Riders.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Road Runners MC
edges.context: Infobox lists Road Runners MC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bandidos
edges.context: Infobox lists Bandidos as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mongols MC
edges.context: Infobox lists Mongols MC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Annihilators MC
edges.context: Infobox lists Annihilators MC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dirty Dozen MC
edges.context: Infobox lists Dirty Dozen MC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vigilantes MC
edges.context: Infobox lists Vigilantes MC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Eleventh Hour RC
edges.context: Infobox lists Eleventh Hour RC as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sons of Silence
edges.context: Infobox lists Sons of Silence as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Vagos MC, (2012–2015)
edges.context: Infobox lists Vagos MC as a rival from 2012 to 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1999, had established its Chatham chapter (based near St. Thomas), when they absorbed the Annihilators Motorcycle Club led by Wayne Kellestine and Giovanni Muscedere.
edges.context: The Loners absorbed the Annihilators Motorcycle Club in 1999.

edges.evidence_quote: To the authorities in Ontario it had been made clear that the Loners and Hells Angels were now at war.
edges.context: The Loners and Hells Angels were at war during the Ontario Biker War.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws would also join the conflict, fighting in the cities of London and Hamilton.
edges.context: The Outlaws fought alongside the Loners against the Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: Atkinson had connections with many group in Ireland, including the Irish Alliance.
edges.context: The Loners had connections with the Irish Alliance and tried to recruit them.

edges.evidence_quote: the Loners Motorcycle Club aligned themselves with fellow Canadian motorcycle club.
edges.context: The Loners allied with the Rock Machine against the Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: the Loners maintained long-time relationships with Bandidos in Europe.
edges.context: The Loners had long-time relationships with the Bandidos in Europe.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, several members of the Loners in Ontario and Western Canada joined the California-based Mongols Motorcycle Club.
edges.context: Several Loners members joined the Mongols in 2007.

edges.evidence_quote: The Loners were determined to compete the American-based club that exile Pammett was now aligning with.
edges.context: The Loners were in conflict with the Vagos over territory in Peterborough.

edges.evidence_quote: On December 1, 2000, the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club became an official probationary club of the Bandidos.
edges.context: The Rock Machine became a probationary club of the Bandidos in 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: The only gang affiliated with the Hells Angels in Ontario is the Para-Dice Riders.
edges.context: The Para-Dice Riders were affiliated with the Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, a 47-year-old member of the Loners Motorcycle Club was sent to hospital with a broken arm after being attacked by several former Loners, who were now affiliated with the Vagos.
edges.context: Loners and Vagos were in conflict, with attacks and arsons between them.

edges.evidence_quote: By absorbing the local Vigilantes Motorcycle Club, which had acted as their support club, this was done in a patch over ceremony that happened in September 2012.
edges.context: Loners absorbed the Vigilantes as a support club in a patch-over ceremony.

edges.evidence_quote: The Loners-Vagos conflict would come to a close due to the patch over of the Vagos Peterborough chapter to the Outlaws Motorcycle Club in 2015. A club that had historically good to moderate relations with the Loners.
edges.context: Loners had historically good to moderate relations with the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: They (the Loners) have a portion of the market and do work in conjunction with other elements of organized crime, including the Italian Mafia.
edges.context: Loners work in conjunction with the Italian Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Police had also temporarily confiscated the 'cuts' of Loners MC Nomads chapter belonging to the individual that was arrested and a Dirty Dozen MC 'cut', which is one of the Loners support clubs in Canada.
edges.context: Dirty Dozen MC is a support club for the Loners Motorcycle Club in Canada.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws and Loners had historically been allies, especially in Canada. During the late 1990s, the Outlaws supplied narcotics to the Loners and the two groups were allied against the Hells Angels in the Ontario Biker War.
edges.context: Historically, Outlaws supplied narcotics to Loners and they were allied against Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: Three members of the Outlaws Motorcycle Club were arrested and charged in what authorities referred to as 'linked to ongoing tensions between the Outlaws Motorcycle Club and the Loners Motorcycle Club.'
edges.context: Ongoing tensions and violent incidents between Loners and Outlaws in 2022-2023.

edges.evidence_quote: During the late 1990s, the Outlaws supplied narcotics to the Loners and the two groups were allied against the Hells Angels in the Ontario Biker War.
edges.context: Loners were allied with Outlaws against Hells Angels in the Ontario Biker War.

edges.evidence_quote: When the Loners Peterborough chapter had a conflict with the Vagos Motorcycle Club Canada in 2012–2014, tensions between the two groups ended when the Vagos were absorbed by the returning Outlaws.
edges.context: Loners Peterborough chapter had a conflict with Vagos Canada from 2012-2014.
